system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: Porcupines do not actively attack. Instead, they defend themselves using their quills. When threatened, a porcupine will turn its back to the predator, raise its quills, and lash its tail. The quills are loosely attached and can easily detach when they come into contact with another animal. Contrary to popular belief, porcupines cannot shoot their quills, but the quills can embed in the skin of a predator if they make contact.
